summary: Addresses external sourcery-ai[bot] feedback on CC-0087: (1) owner-ref matching in secretToKeystoneMapper now compares only the API group (via schema.ParseGroupVersion) instead of the full APIVersion string, so Secrets persisted with an older APIVersion survive a future CRD version bump; (2) owner-ref path performs a cached client.Get on the referenced Keystone and drops NotFound results while letting non-NotFound errors fall through to enqueue, guarding against spurious/stale ownerRefs without dropping legitimate events on transient cache blips; (3) documentation at docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md updated to reflect group-only matching, the existence check, and the API-server/API server terminology nit. Five new unit tests pin the group-only match, foreign-group rejection, malformed APIVersion skip, stale Keystone skip, and transient-error enqueue. The keystonePaths() error-handling nit from sourcery-ai is declined with a multi-line REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comment (reviewer misread `_` as a discarded error; the function returns two strings, no error).

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./internal/controller/... green on the new mapper tests)
Edge cases are covered — group match / foreign group / malformed APIVersion / stale Keystone / transient Get error
Existing TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_* tests still pass without assertion changes
Integration tests reuse registerSecretNameIndex — not touched in this round, still satisfied

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing patterns: log-and-swallow for mapper errors, named helper constants, handler.MapFunc contract
Uses schema.ParseGroupVersion (standard apimachinery) rather than ad-hoc string splitting
Cached Get pattern documented inline with rationale (in-memory, no API-server round-trip)
All comments in English (CLAUDE critical-rules rule #1 LANGUAGE)

security_checklist.item: No new external input paths introduced — mapper operates on informer-cache objects

Owner-ref forgery: group-only matching still restricts to keystone.openstack.c5c3.io; foreign-group refs are explicitly rejected and covered by TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_OwnerRefDifferentGroupIgnored
No secret material logged or exposed

architecture_checklist.item: Owner-ref group match via schema.ParseGroupVersion is the canonical approach used across the K8s ecosystem (matches the new review pattern avoid-exact-apiversion-matching-on-owner-references.md)
Cached existence guard matches the new review pattern verify-referenced-objects-exist-before-enqueueing.md and its instruction to treat non-NotFound as enqueue
Solution is minimal — diff scope confined to the mapper, its doc comment, tests, and the reconciler reference doc

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — single ParseGroupVersion path, single Get guard
No speculative generality — only the two cases the external reviewer raised are addressed

fail_fast_checklist.item: Parse errors and foreign groups short-circuit via continue at the top of the loop
NotFound on the cached Get is treated as a definitive signal to skip (fail-fast drop); other errors deliberately do not fail the event

defensive_checklist.item: Malformed APIVersion is handled without panic (TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_OwnerRefMalformedAPIVersionIgnored)

issues_found.description: Non-English text introduced in a source-code comment. operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go:276 ends the REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT block with 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' ('bitte prüfen' is German for 'please verify'). Violates the project's critical-rules rule #1 LANGUAGE: 'All output in English (code, comments, commits, docs)'. This exact phrasing has already been flagged as a defect in prior reviews on this repository (CC-0013, CC-0047, CC-0059, CC-0065) — it is a recurring anti-pattern, not a one-off.
issues_found.fix: Replace 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' with 'Reviewer: please verify.' — or drop the trailing sentence entirely, since the preceding 'No change required; keeping the blank discard with this explanatory comment to prevent re-misreads.' already closes the block.

issues_found.description: The REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comment at envtest_setup.go:267-276 is 10 lines of process meta-commentary attached to a single `crdDir, _ := keystonePaths()` line. The WHY is valid (prevent future reviewers from re-misreading `_` as a discarded error), but at this length it dominates the function and mixes review-cycle context (CC-0087 review #1, sourcery-ai[bot]) with the actual technical explanation. Once 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' is removed, consider tightening to ~3 lines: what the reviewer thought, why it was wrong, that fail-fast is already covered by ErrorIfCRDPathMissing.
issues_found.fix: Tighten to approximately: '// keystonePaths() returns (crdDir, webhookDir string); the blank discards webhookDir (SetupMinimalEnvTest does not install webhooks). envtest.Environment{ErrorIfCRDPathMissing: true} below fails fast if crdDir is absent.' This keeps the re-misread guard without the review-cycle meta.

suggested_improvements: Optionally log non-NotFound Get errors in the owner-ref path at V(1) so persistent cache issues (e.g. scheme mismatches) are diagnosable. Current silent fall-through is correct per the new pattern but offers no observability signal — a one-line log.FromContext(ctx).V(1).Info("cached Get error, enqueuing owner-ref anyway", "err", err, "key", key) would close that gap without changing behavior.

Consider a standing lint or pre-commit grep (e.g. `git diff | grep -nE '[äöüßÄÖÜ]|bitte prüfen'`) to catch the recurring German-phrase-in-comments anti-pattern before PR review. Five reviews (CC-0013, CC-0047, CC-0059, CC-0065, CC-0087) now show the same failure mode.
next_steps: Delete or translate 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' at operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go:276 — single-line AUTO_FIX.
Re-run go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/... and go vet ./operators/keystone/... to confirm no regressions after the comment edit.
Optionally tighten the surrounding REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT block (ASK).
